name: railway
description: Inspect and control Railway projects, services, and deployments

envVars.name: RAILWAY_TOKEN
envVars.description: Railway account or workspace token (Account Settings > Tokens)

tools.name: List Projects
tools.description: List Railway projects accessible to the token

endpoint.query: query { projects { edges { node { id name description createdAt updatedAt } } } }

historicalSummary.omitted: project settings and provider-specific payload fields

tools.name: Get Project
tools.description: Get a project with its services and environments

endpoint.query: query($id: String!) { project(id: $id) { id name description createdAt services { edges { node { id name icon } } } environments { edges { node { id name } } } } }

tools.name: List Deployments
tools.description: List deployments filtered by project, service, or environment

endpoint.query: query($projectId: String, $serviceId: String, $environmentId: String, $first: Int) { deployments(input: { projectId: $projectId, serviceId: $serviceId, environmentId: $environmentId }, first: $first) { edges { node { id status createdAt url staticUrl } } } }

historicalSummary.omitted: deployment metadata and provider-specific payload fields

tools.name: Get Deployment
tools.description: Get a deployment by ID, including redeploy/rollback eligibility

endpoint.query: query($id: String!) { deployment(id: $id) { id status createdAt url staticUrl meta canRedeploy canRollback } }

tools.name: Redeploy Deployment
tools.description: Redeploy an existing deployment (creates a new deployment from it)

prompts.title: Deployment health
prompts.prompt: List the latest Railway deployments for my project and summarize their statuses.

prompts.title: Restart a deployment
prompts.prompt: Find the active deployment of my Railway service and restart it.

SETUP_GUIDE.title: Railway Public API Setup

steps.title: Sign in to Railway
steps.description: Go to https://railway.com and sign in (the trial/Hobby plan with one deployed project is sufficient for testing).

steps.title: Create an API token
steps.description: Open Account Settings > Tokens (https://railway.com/account/tokens) and create an account token, or select a workspace to create a workspace-scoped token.

steps.title: Set the environment variable
steps.description: Add the token to your .env as RAILWAY_TOKEN=...

steps.title: Verify access
steps.description: Run List Projects, then use a project ID with Get Project to discover its service and environment IDs.

notes: The API is GraphQL-only at https://backboard.railway.com/graphql/v2; account and workspace tokens are sent as 'Authorization: Bearer <token>'
Project tokens use a different header (Project-Access-Token) and are not supported by this connector — use an account or workspace token
Results use Relay-style connections; each item is wrapped in an edge with a node field
